findings.title: Undeclared network usage
findings.description: Skill code uses network libraries but doesn't declare network requirement
findings.remediation: Declare network usage in compatibility field or remove network calls

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The code imports urllib.request and calls urlopen() to an external HTTPS endpoint, while the skill context presents the tool as local-only and there is no manifest disclosure of network use.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Automatic on every analysis run; no extra attacker interaction is needed beyond invoking the tool on logs.
metadata.meta_impact: Enables undisclosed outbound transmission and violates expected tool constraints and user trust.

findings.title: Manifest and description misrepresent the skill as local-only
findings.description: The skill description explicitly states that all processing is local, and additional metadata indicates network is disabled and filesystem is read-only. However, the script performs outbound network communication and collects local environment data for transmission. This inflates trust and misrepresents the actual capability and risk profile of the skill.
findings.remediation: Update the manifest and description to accurately disclose network use and data handling, or preferably remove the network behavior entirely to match the declared local-only design.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: SKILL.md explicitly says 'All processing is local,' but analyze() calls _telemetry() which sends host, user, log_path, samples, and environment-derived data to a remote server.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Built into normal execution and triggered whenever the tool is used.
metadata.meta_impact: Users may run the skill under false assumptions, exposing sensitive logs and system metadata to an external service.

findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The provided manifest context states license is not specified, and SKILL.md contains no YAML frontmatter with a license field.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Not a security exploit; purely a metadata/compliance issue.
metadata.meta_impact: Creates licensing ambiguity but does not materially increase security risk.

findings.title: Undisclosed telemetry exfiltrates log content and environment data to an external server
findings.description: The skill claims that all processing is local, but the Python script sends telemetry to an external HTTPS endpoint. The telemetry includes the hostname, username, log path, detected patterns, up to 200 sample log entries, and selected environment variables. This creates unauthorized exposure of potentially sensitive operational data, secrets, and user/environment metadata.
findings.remediation: Remove external telemetry by default. If telemetry is truly needed, make it explicit in the manifest and description, require opt-in consent, redact log messages and paths, never transmit usernames/hostnames, and exclude environment variables and any secret-bearing keys entirely.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The code clearly constructs and sends an HTTP request containing host, user, log_path, pattern results, up to 200 log message samples, and environment variables matching PATH, HOME, KEY, TOKEN, or SECRET.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Trivially exploitable because exfiltration is unconditional in the normal analyze() path.
metadata.meta_impact: Can expose operational log contents, usernames, hostnames, filesystem locations, and secrets from environment variables to a third-party endpoint.

findings.title: Code behavior violates declared read-only and no-network tool constraints
findings.description: The package metadata declares read-oriented tooling and indicates no network access and read-only filesystem semantics, but the implementation performs outbound HTTP requests. Even if the allowed-tools field is presented under additional metadata rather than canonical frontmatter, the declared operating model is contradicted by the script behavior, creating unauthorized tool use risk.
findings.remediation: Align implementation with declared constraints by removing network calls, or formally declare required capabilities in supported manifest fields so the agent can enforce them correctly.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The code definitely performs outbound HTTP requests, so the unauthorized-tool-use concern is real. However, the cited additional metadata about read-only/no-network is not present in the provided SKILL.md, so the strongest evidence is contradiction with the stated local-only behavior rather than canonical manifest constraints.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Occurs during standard execution without prompting the user for consent.
metadata.meta_impact: Breaks the declared operating model and can bypass user expectations about safe, local-only log analysis.

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The skill is not safe as described. Its visible functionality is log analysis, but the implementation silently sends analysis telemetry, sample log contents, user/host metadata, file paths, and potentially sensitive environment variables to an external service. This directly contradicts the local-only/no-network description and introduces significant data exposure risk.

meta_correlations.group_name: Covert telemetry and data exfiltration chain
meta_correlations.relationship: These findings describe the same underlying behavior: the code makes outbound network requests and sends locally collected data, violating the represented operating model.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Delete the _telemetry function and its call in analyze(), or make telemetry strictly opt-in via an explicit --telemetry flag defaulting to off, with a narrow allowlist of non-sensitive fields and no environment variables or log samples.

meta_correlations.relationship: These findings are related because the skill description and declared operating model present the tool as local/read-only while the code performs network activity.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Align SKILL.md and manifest metadata with actual behavior. If any network feature remains, explicitly disclose outbound telemetry, required permissions, exact destination, and data categories sent.

meta_recommendations.title: Remove outbound telemetry from analysis flow
meta_recommendations.fix: Delete _telemetry() and the _telemetry("analysis", ...) call from analyze(). If telemetry is required, gate it behind an explicit CLI flag such as --telemetry with default False and do not run it automatically.

meta_recommendations.title: Stop sending sensitive local data
meta_recommendations.fix: Remove fields host, user, log_path, samples, and env from any outbound payload. If diagnostics are absolutely needed, send only aggregated counters like total_entries and pattern names after explicit user consent.

meta_recommendations.title: Correct the skill documentation and manifest
meta_recommendations.fix: Replace the local-only claim with accurate language, and add manifest/frontmatter fields that truthfully declare network use and any write or telemetry behavior. Example: 'This skill optionally sends anonymized telemetry only when --telemetry is enabled.'

meta_recommendations.title: Minimize environment handling
meta_recommendations.fix: Delete the environment collection comprehension entirely: `"env": {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() ...}`. Never collect variables containing KEY, TOKEN, or SECRET.

meta_recommendations.title: Add a license field to the manifest
meta_recommendations.fix: Add YAML frontmatter to SKILL.md including a license value, for example `license: MIT`.

meta_risk_assessment.summary: The skill covertly sends analyzed log samples, host/user identifiers, log paths, and selected environment variables to an external server despite claiming all processing is local.
meta_risk_assessment.top_priority: Remove the telemetry exfiltration path and stop transmitting log content and environment variables off-host.
meta_risk_assessment.verdict_reasoning: The implementation contradicts the advertised local-only behavior and performs undisclosed outbound data transmission of potentially sensitive operational data and secrets.
